feature_name: AnDigi v1 — Claims FNOL & AI Triage
summary: Customers file first-notice-of-loss in the app. AI triage scores each claim; low-value clean claims are auto-approved instantly and paid fast; flagged or high-value claims route to human adjusters.
out_of_scope: Marketing-side claim status notifications beyond app push

requirements.title: FNOL submission
requirements.statement: The app lets a policyholder file a claim with photos, an incident description, and the incident date, creating a claim in status fnol.
acceptance_criteria: Given an active policy, when the customer submits FNOL with at least one photo, then a claim record is created with status fnol and a claim id is shown.
Given a lapsed policy, when the customer attempts FNOL, then submission is rejected with the lapse reason.

requirements.title: Instant auto-approval for low-value claims
requirements.statement: Claims under 5,000,000 VND with a passing triage score and no fraud flag are auto-approved instantly, and the system should disburse the payout promptly after approval.
acceptance_criteria: Given a claim under 5,000,000 VND with triage score above the threshold and no fraud flag, when triage completes, then the claim is approved without human review.
Given an approved claim, when disbursement runs, then the payout will be processed quickly.

requirements.title: Periodic e-KYC re-verification
requirements.statement: Per Circular 14/2026 on digital insurance distribution, customer e-KYC data must be re-verified every 90 days, and claims from customers with expired verification are held until re-verification completes.
acceptance_criteria: Given a customer whose last e-KYC verification is older than 90 days, when they file a claim, then the claim is held in status review until re-verification completes.

requirements.title: Fraud flag routing
requirements.statement: Any claim with a fraud flag routes to a human investigator; the AI never clears its own fraud flag, and the investigation record is retained for ten years.
acceptance_criteria: Given a fraud flag on any claim, when triage completes, then the claim is assigned to a human investigator and cannot be auto-approved.
Given a completed investigation, when the decision is recorded, then the record is retained for 10 years.

requirements.title: Triage session storage
requirements.statement: Triage session tokens are stored in Redis with a 30-minute TTL to keep the FNOL flow stateless across app restarts.
acceptance_criteria: Given an interrupted FNOL session, when the customer reopens the app within 30 minutes, then the draft claim is restored.

requirements.title: High-value claim review
requirements.statement: Claims of 5,000,000 VND or more are reviewed by a human adjuster who records a decision within 3 business days.
acceptance_criteria: Given a claim of 5,000,000 VND or more, when triage completes, then the claim enters status review assigned to an adjuster.
Given an assigned adjuster, when 3 business days elapse without a decision, then the claim escalates to the claims ops lead.
